verification_summary: pytest passed with 9 tests. The CMD findstr port lookup pattern was checked. stop.bat was not executed because ports 8000 and 5173 were already occupied by running local processes.

implemented: Added src/start.bat to start backend and frontend from src-relative directories in separate CMD windows.
Added src/stop.bat to find LISTENING PIDs on ports 8000 and 5173 and terminate them with taskkill.
Added pytest coverage for the batch file contract.
risks: stop.bat terminates by port, so an unrelated local process on port 8000 or 5173 can be stopped.
Full manual start and browser verification remains for the verify stage.
future_improvements: If needed, stop.bat can later add working-directory filtering to reduce unrelated process termination risk.
decisions: Kept the scope to Windows .bat files under src and did not add PowerShell or root-level helper scripts.
Used %~dp0 so the scripts work regardless of the current shell directory.
Avoided executing stop.bat during development because the target ports were already occupied by local processes.
